preview_text:
Argentina enters as a clear favourite with a 69% win probability according to the model, leaving Switzerland just 7% to claim victory. The gap between the two reflects a substantial disparity in the statistical forecast, though Switzerland is not without a path to the upset.

The draw sits at 23% in the model's view, suggesting a competitive match where a stalemate represents a meaningful outcome. The win probabilities are separated enough that Argentina holds a decisive edge, yet the presence of a sizeable draw probability indicates the match carries genuine uncertainty rather than a foregone conclusion.

The model and sportsbooks diverge notably on this fixture. The market assigns Argentina only 56% to win—13 percentage points lower than the model—while awarding Switzerland 18% versus the model's 7%. Books price the draw marginally higher at 27%. These gaps suggest material disagreement on the likely outcome; the dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is more accurate.
